ref: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.538, p.24 (2023)
title: Measurement of thick target neutron yield from 80.5 MeV/u 12C incidence on Be, C, W, and Pb targets

x4freetext: Measurement of thick target neutron yield from 80.5
MeV/u 12C incidence on Be, C, W, and Pb targets

x4codes.hlp: Institute of Modern Physics, CAS, Lanzhou, China, People`s Republic

x4freetext: Advanced Energy Science and Technology
Guangdong Laboratory, Huizhou

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.538, p.24 (2023)

x4codes.hlp: Institute of Modern Physics, CAS, Lanzhou, China, People`s Republic

x4freetext: The Radioactive Ion Beam Line in
Lanzhou (RIBLL)
The 12C ions with 80.5 MeV/u
Secondary particles produced in the target and
reached the dE - E telescope after passing through a
5-mm thick iron window at the end of the vacuum
chamber.
The QDCs with the total and slow gates were used
to separate the photon and neutron events with
pulse-shape discrimination (PSD) method.
Counts of incident beam particles were
continuously monitored by a 0.5 mm thick plastic
scintillator detector which was placed at 67 cm
upstream from the target in a vacuum chamber.
The dE - E telescope was consisted
of three liquid scintillator detectors and three
plastic detectors. Three BC501 liquid scintillator
detectors (12.7 cm diameter and 12.7 cm thick) were
kept at a distance of 1.5 m for 0 deg and 1.0 m for
37 deg and 60 deg with respect to the beam direction
in the laboratory frame and were used as E counter.
The distances were defined from the target center to
the surface of each liquid scintillation detector.
Three EJ212 plastic detectors (12 x 12 cm square and
0.5 cm thick) placed in front of the E counter were
utilized as veto detectors to discriminate charged
particles from neutrons and photons and were used as
dE counter.
Total uncertainty
The statistical errors vary from 2
to 5 % for the low to medium energy about 10 - 100
MeV region and increase to about 20% at the highest
energies of the spectra.
The uncertainty of the detection efficiency
is estimated to be less than 10 % for the incident
neutron-energy range of 0.1 - 80 MeV, and slightly
exceeds 10 % with higher energy neutrons.
A value of 15% as the uncertainty of the
total errors in the data analysis process.
